t_ch: 扮演跳动的<color=#f29e38ff>广告牌</color>，想办法<color=#f29e38ff>躲避哈努兄弟的视线</color>！
t_en: Play the role of a bouncing <color=#f29e38ff>Billboard</color> and figure out a way to <color=#f29e38ff>avoid Brother Hanu's gaze</color>!

t_ch: 当哈努兄弟转头时，<color=#f29e38ff>长按屏幕</color>来停止跳动。

t_en: Now prepare to start evasion proper!

t_ch: 现在是「一、二、三…广告牌！」的进阶试炼。
t_en: You're doing the "One, Two, Three... Billboards!" Advanced Trial right now.

t_ch: 先来复习一下节奏吧。
t_en: Let's rehearse the beat first.

t_ch: 材料都在吧台上，种类齐全，你直接用就好。不收钱。
t_en: The ingredients are all on the lounge, the whole range. Feel free to use them. I won't charge you.

t_ch: 噢，差点忘了，要是客人们满意的话，我会按工作量给你付酬劳的。待遇包你满意。
t_en: Oh, I almost forgot. I'll pay you based on your workload if the customers are happy. The pay will satisfy you for sure.

t_ch: 我会在对面守着，放心大胆地去做吧。
t_en: I'll be watching things from over there. Take it easy and dive into your work.

t_ch: （…记得在饮品单里看到过这杯饮料。）
t_en: (...I remember seeing this drink on the drink menu.)

t_ch: （开始调制）
t_en: (Start preparing the drink)

t_ch: 启动调饮模拟器！
t_en: Activate the drinksmith simulator!

t_ch: （感觉黑桃说话有点疯疯的，但还算是好接触的类型？）
t_en: (I feel like the way Spade talks is a bit crazy, but they seem pretty easy to get along with.)

t_ch: 记录「黑桃」的情况。
t_en: Record information about Spade.

t_ch: 嘿呀。让我瞧瞧…新人？该不会…你是欠了舒翁的债才被抓过来打工的吧？
t_en: Heya! Let's take a look at this... new drinksmith? Let me guess... You owe Siobhan some money, which is how you got roped into working here.

t_ch: 肯定是这样！我就知道，舒翁看起来温声细语，待人温柔又好说话，走在街上都要被问有没有兴趣去当明星的那种，但背地里绝对是个魔鬼！
t_en: That must be it! I knew it. Siobhan looks soft-spoken and treats people so gently. She's quite the talker. Whenever she's on the street, people always ask if she wants to become a celebrity, but she's definitely a devil in disguise!

t_ch: 直接说吧，你欠了她多少钱？看看…嘿，我能不能帮你找补找补？
t_en: Be straight with me. How much do you owe her? Let's see... Hey, can I help you with that?

t_ch: 你会这么好心？
t_en: You'd do that for me?

t_ch: 恐怕还不起噢。
t_en: Even with your help, I still might not be able to pay it back.

t_ch: 不会吧，你不会真的以为我会帮你还钱吧。我是说…我知道有个发财的地。
t_en: Are you kidding me? You didn't really think I was going to help you pay off your debt? I was saying... I know a place where you can strike it rich.

t_ch: 梦境酒店的深处，嘻嘻，那里有个长得像闹钟的怪物，就喜欢别人骂它。你呢，过去使劲多喷它几句，说不定…它心情好，就把钱全给你了！
t_en: In the back of the hotel in the Dreamscape, hehe, there's a monster that looks like an alarm clock and enjoys having people tell it off. So you could go over and spit a few sentences at it. Maybe... that'll put it in a good mood, and then it'll give you all its money!

t_ch: 记住了吧，可别说是我告诉你的哦。嘎嘎嘎嘎…好了，做个乖酒保，给我点喝的。
t_en: Remember, you can't say that I was the one who told you. Hahaha... Okay. Now, be a good drinksmith and put in an order for me.

t_ch: 来点苦的怎么样？让我想想，想…就要那个！那个…那个…<color=#dbc291ff>迷失午夜</color>！
t_en: Let's get the bitter ones! Let me think, I think... I want that! That... that... <color=#dbc291ff>Lost Twilight</color>!

t_ch: 哇嘎嘎嘎！这饮料的颜色就很适合我，你看，我多深沉啊，沉得都飞起来了！
t_en: Woah woah woah! The color of the drink suits me so well. Look at how deep and profound I am. So deep that I'm flying!

t_ch: 刚才说到哪儿了？噢，梦境酒店，那地方可不太平。
t_en: What was I saying? Oh, the hotel in the Dreamscape is not safe.

t_ch: 舒翁不太让咱们随便走远，但我偷偷给你讲，前天啊，我在那看到了好多人。
t_en: Siobhan doesn't let us go too far, but just between us, I saw lots of people over there a few days ago.

t_ch: 就是那种穿…穿啥来着…黑色西装的！碰见就想逮住我，吓得我都掉小珍珠了。结果你猜…怎么着，他们根本就不敢进来哈哈！
t_en: They were all wearing... something like... black suits! They wanted to arrest me when they saw me. I was so shocked I got teary-eyed. And in the end, guess what... they don't even dare to step foot in here. Hahaha!

t_ch: 我站在里面疯狂嘲讽，他们气得直冒烟呢，只能灰溜溜地走了。你说有意思不！
t_en: I stood inside and started wildly mocking them. They were so mad that smoke was coming out of their ears. All they could do was walk away with their tails between their legs. Pretty interesting, right?

t_ch: 好像有点口渴…再给我来杯味道<color=#dbc291ff>超级强烈</color>的饮料吧，要能让我回忆起戏耍他们的愉悦的那种！
t_en: I'm a little thirsty... Bring me another <color=#dbc291ff>super intense</color> drink, one that reminds me of the joys of playing tricks on them!

t_ch: 嘿，朋友，知道嘛，你都让我汗流浃背了。我说你该不会是走后门进来的吧？
t_en: Hey, friend. You know, you've got me breaking out in a sweat. Did you get a position here cos you know someone?

t_ch: 算了！别看我个头小，心里头却是一等一地宽容！
t_en: Forget it! I may be small, but I have a big and tolerant heart!

t_ch: 刚才说到哪儿了？噢，梦境酒店，那地方可不太平。
t_en: What was I saying? Oh, the hotel in the Dreamscape is not safe.

t_ch: 舒翁不太让咱们随便走远，但我偷偷给你讲，前天啊，我在那看到了好多人。
t_en: Siobhan doesn't let us go too far, but just between us, I saw lots of people over there a few days ago.

t_ch: 就是那种穿…穿啥来着…黑色西装的！碰见就想逮住我，吓得我都掉小珍珠了。结果你猜…怎么着，他们根本就不敢进来哈哈！
t_en: They were all wearing... something like... black suits! They wanted to arrest me when they saw me. I was so shocked I got teary-eyed. And in the end, guess what... they don't even dare to step foot in here. Hahaha!

t_ch: 我站在里面疯狂嘲讽，他们气得直冒烟呢，只能灰溜溜地走了。你说有意思不！
t_en: I stood inside and started wildly mocking them. They were so mad that smoke was coming out of their ears. All they could do was walk away with their tails between their legs. Pretty interesting, right?

t_ch: 好像有点口渴…再给我来杯味道<color=#dbc291ff>超级强烈</color>的饮料吧，要能让我回忆起戏耍他们的愉悦的那种！
t_en: I'm a little thirsty... Bring me another <color=#dbc291ff>super intense</color> drink, one that reminds me of the joys of playing tricks on them!

t_ch: 你做的好啊！喝这个仿佛就能体会到那些黑西装的苦涩心情，就像…呜呜，哭着回去找妈妈的那种感觉哈哈哈哈哈！
t_en: You did a good job! Drinking this, it's like I can feel the bitter emotions of those people in black suits. It's like... *sob* going home crying to your mother. Hahaha!

t_ch: 说了这么多话，感觉舒坦了不少。再差的酒保也是个酒保，总能陪你聊两句不是。
t_en: With all that said, I feel way more comfortable. I guess even a bad drinksmith is still a drinksmith who can spare you a word or two.

t_ch: 嗝。差不多饱了…走了走了。新人，你自己多保重！可别被舒翁吃了哦。
t_en: *hiccup* I think I've had enough... I'm off. Take care of yourself, newbie! Don't let that Siobhan eat you alive.

t_ch: 这是啥？还是我舌头出问题了？再尝一口……
t_en: What's this? Or is there something wrong with my taste buds? Let me taste it again...

t_ch: 喂，你该不会是个味盲吧？醋和糖浆都分不出来的那种，对着陌生人喊妈妈的那种？
t_en: Hey, are you unable to taste flavors, or something? You know, the kind of person who can't tell vinegar from sugar? Like the kind of person who would call a complete stranger "Mom"?

t_ch: 说了这么多话，感觉舒坦了不少。再差的酒保也是个酒保，总能陪你聊两句不是。
t_en: With all that said, I feel way more comfortable. I guess even a bad drinksmith is still a drinksmith who can spare you a word or two.

t_ch: 嗝。差不多饱了…走了走了。新人，你自己多保重！可别被舒翁吃了哦。
t_en: *hiccup* I think I've had enough... I'm off. Take care of yourself, newbie! Don't let that Siobhan eat you alive.

t_ch: 嘿，亲爱的朋友，不要溜走，本琥珀纪最惊艳的魔术表演即将拉开序幕！
t_en: Hey, my dear friend! Stick around, because the most mind-blowing magic show in this Amber Era is about to start!

t_ch: 或许，你已经见识过我身后这神奇的迷宫——我敢打包票，没人能在里面找到出口。
t_en: You might have noticed the incredible maze behind me. No one will ever find their way out of it.

t_ch: 而现在，表演就要开始，请耐心参与其中。
t_en: And now, the show is about to begin. Sit tight and join in.

t_ch: 去吧，燃起你的热血！当最后的三块拼图被补齐，我便会将迷宫的面貌改写。而魔术师的秘宝，亦将为你显形！
t_en: Get ready for a thrilling adventure! When the last three puzzle pieces fall into place, I'll reshape the maze, and the magician's secret treasure will be revealed to you!

t_ch: 可真是堵厚障壁，一眼望去，恐怕得凑齐整整十二位英雄的伟力才能将它洞开。
t_en: These walls are seriously strong. It would probably take a dozen heroes to break through them.

t_ch: 坚不可摧的事物总是从内部瓦解…从高处俯瞰世界，也许能找到开辟道路的谜题。
t_en: What seems impenetrable always cracks from within... Look from above, and you might find the puzzle that opens the path.

t_ch: 到全新的迷宫中寻找吧！我把财宝都藏在了那里——你仿佛能听到魔术师的耳语，他在期待赞美与掌声。
t_en: "Seek it out in a whole new maze! I've hidden all my treasures there!" It feels like you are hearing the magician's whisper, eager for praise and applause.

t_ch: 就这？本想对他的节目指点一二，但你忙着去找他的宝箱，还是算了。
t_en: Is that it? I wanted to give him some advice on his show, but you were too busy searching for his treasure chest, so forget it.

t_ch: 英雄，总算把您给盼来了，您要是再晚会儿，他们非得把脑袋给我削下来不可。
t_en: You finally made it here! If you didn't show up, they would've had my head.

t_ch: 也不知道是哪个家伙在公馆里搞的破坏，触发了建造之初设计的反入侵装置，办公室里的空间被弄得一团糟。
t_en: Someone messed up the Pavilion and triggered the security system we installed during construction. The office is a complete mess now.

t_ch: 装置的维修要很长一段时间，但里面的文件都有急用，必须得赶快抢救出来。
t_en: It'll take a while to fix the system, but the documents inside are urgently needed and must be salvaged quickly.

t_ch: 面试呢？
t_en: What about the interview?

t_ch: 来的就您一个人，还面什么试啊？直接上吧。
t_en: You're the only one here. Let's just get started.

t_ch: 那些贪生怕死的家伙，一听到可能要和迷因打交道，我话都没说完就没影了。
t_en: Before I could even finish my words, those cowards just ran away as soon as they heard they might run into memes.

t_ch: 看到钱眼睛比激光炮还直，看到危险跑得比扑满还快，还得是英雄您威武豪迈。
t_en: Their eyes lit up like lasers when they heard about money, and they ran faster than a Trotter when they heard about danger. Now I'm counting on you, hero.

t_ch: 至于委托的报酬，说出来怕吓到您——可以直接兑换等额信用点的<unbreak>5000</unbreak>苜蓿币，怎样，没见过这么阔绰的手笔吧？
t_en: Now, for the reward... Brace yourself because this good news might shock you! It's <unbreak>5,000</unbreak> Alfalfa credits! Enough to redeem the same amount of IPC credits! Incredible, right?

t_ch: 你打发谁呢？
t_en: Who do you think I am, a beggar?

t_ch: 得加钱，兄弟。
t_en: To be honest, you'll have to pay more.

t_ch: 这价位很不错了，其他同类的委托更低，<unbreak>5000</unbreak>还是因为在公馆才加的钱。放别人那儿，就这，还得再黑走您两千，五五分成已经不能再良心了。
t_en: That's a decent price. Other agents pay even less! You'll get <unbreak>5,000</unbreak> credits because we're in this Pavilion, and if it were someone else, they'd take another <unbreak>2,000</unbreak> credits from you! A <unbreak>50</unbreak>/<unbreak>50</unbreak> split like this is good enough.

t_ch: 这样，最后到手的钱我三你七，您拿七千，总行了吧？
t_en: Alright then, when everything's settled, I'll take <unbreak>30%</unbreak>, and you take <unbreak>70%</unbreak>. Deal?

t_ch: 时间不等人，上面的雇主们开始催进度了。看到我身后那扇门了吗？准备好了就出发吧。
t_en: Clock's ticking, and the higher-ups want some progress. See that door behind me? Set off whenever you're ready.

t_ch: 英雄，咱们辛辛苦苦跑了这么久，这些文件上都写了些什么，您就不好奇？
t_en: We've been working our butts off, hero. Aren't you curious about what's written on these papers?

t_ch: 只要偷偷看一眼，无论是敲他们一笔，还是卖给其他感兴趣的人，都大有搞头。
t_en: Let's just sneak a peek, and then we could either sell them to someone interested or use the content to blackmail those higher-ups.

t_ch: 猎犬还兴这个？
t_en: Are the Bloodhounds into this, too?

t_ch: 我可从没说过自己是猎犬，咱就一打工的。
t_en: Hey, I never said I was a Bloodhound. I'm just an ordinary employee trying to make ends meet.

t_ch: 上头把委托交给猎犬，猎犬家系再把任务交到底下，底下的又把工作外包给我们，我们再替他们招人来干活，拿点「小费」很正常。
t_en: The Bloodhounds get these tasks from the top brass and then assign them to their agents. The agents then outsource the work to us, and then we help recruit people like you to handle it, so it's only fair that we take a little "tip" for ourselves.

t_ch: 欸？这几份文件…是不是有点？《美梦剧团的生产后护理》、《苏乐达的一百种喝法》，他们平时都看的啥啊。
t_en: Huh? These documents... seriously? Postpartum Care for Sweet Dreams Troupe, and A Hundred Ways to Drink SoulGlad...? What the heck are they reading in their leisure time?

t_ch: 英雄，您看，咱要不再进去一趟？外包嘛，不寒碜。
t_en: Maybe we should go in again, hero? Just outsourcing work, nothing big deal.

t_ch: 《关于增加筑梦经费的提案》、《白日梦酒店设备更新草案》，都在张嘴要钱。看来不止是咱们经费不够，上头也没钱，大家都不容易啊。
t_en: Proposal on Increasing Funding for Dream Construction Project, and Draft on Equipment Renewal in Reverie Hotel... all about asking for money. Looks like even the higher-ups are strapped for cash.

t_ch: 不过这回总该没——等等，为什么这份经费报表跟个拼图似的，只有半截…
t_en: But this time, there isn't... Wait, why does this funding statement look like a jigsaw puzzle? It's only half of it...

t_ch: 某种防盗手段？
t_en: Some kind of anti-theft system?

t_ch: 您有没有发现一个盲点？真想偷情报的从来没被防住，咱们这些劳碌命反而次次被绕得原地转圈。
t_en: Noticed something funny? These methods never prevent those who really intend to steal information. They only make us ordinary workers go in circles.

t_ch: 如果安全带只在需要解开的时候才结实，那它到底是安全带还是危险带？
t_en: If a seat belt is only fastened and sturdy when it needs to be unbuckled, is it a seat belt or a stupid belt?

t_ch: 算了，事情都办到这儿了，咱也不好拿着半截文件回去交差。最后回去一次，我保证。
t_en: Well, forget it. We can't just go back with half of the document. We'll go back one last time, I promise.

t_ch: 伯顿小心翼翼地把两份报表放在一起，纸质的文件上，密密麻麻的数字飞速闪动。
t_en: Burton carefully pieces the two parts of the statement together, and the numbers on the paper rapidly flash.

t_ch: 不愧是公馆的东西，这报表的内容居然是实时更新的。
t_en: This stuff updates in real time? As I'd expect from the Pavilion.

t_ch: 趁四下无人，英雄，咱们也来瞅瞅，被上头加急抢救出来的文件，到底有多重要。
t_en: Since there's no one around, let's take a peek at this document and see why the higher-ups are so desperate for it, hero.

t_ch: 这不太好吧？
t_en: Are we really doing this?

t_ch: 让我看看！
t_en: Let me see!

t_ch: 没什么兴趣。
t_en: I'm not interested.

t_ch: 您看，这儿还有批给找回这份文件的经费呢——什么？二十万！
t_en: Look at this, the document reveals the reward amount for those who retrieve it... Wait, WHAT!? <unbreak>200,000</unbreak> credits!?

t_ch: 到猎犬手里剩十五万，再分到底下各个人手里，怎么着也得有个五六万，那小子居然骗我说就到手了三万。
t_en: The Bloodhounds took a chunk, and the agents chipped away some more. In the end, my boss said the reward was only <unbreak>30,000</unbreak> credits.

t_ch: 难怪委托难度这么高，感情是把上头发的钱，都给吃了回扣了！亏我还在替他们哭穷，我呸，再穷也穷不到他们头上。
t_en: Tsk! No wonder this task is so tough! They've taken all the money and left us with a measly payout! And here I thought they were strapped for cash and I was trying to help them save money!

t_ch: 这是您的报酬，一分不少，如果有多的，就当是补偿您的辛苦费。
t_en: Here's your payment, not a penny less. And if it's more than the amount we agreed on, consider it as compensation for your hard work.

t_ch: 至于雇主那边，他胃口倒挺好，今儿个我非得去和他交流交流「感情」不可。
t_en: As for my boss, heh, seems like he's got a big appetite. I'm going to have a "little chat" with him today.

t_ch: 喔唷，实际状况可比我听到的版本的糟糕多了，怎么才给这点儿钱？
t_en: Geez, the actual situation is way worse than what I heard. If that's the case, why is the pay so paltry?

t_ch: 猎犬的误判吗？看来上头的人手确实不够。
t_en: Did the Bloodhounds miscalculate? Looks like they're really understaffed.

t_ch: 这是？跨星域出差旅费的报销单，这下有倒霉蛋要破费了。
t_en: What's this? An interstellar travel reimbursement? Now some poor soul has to foot the bill.

t_ch: 咱们还是再往更深处找找吧。
t_en: Let's dig deeper.

t_ch: 应该翻得差不多了，再找下去我估计得把墙皮都撕下来一层。
t_en: I think we're almost done. If we have to keep digging, then I guess we'll have to tear down the walls.

t_ch: 还是先从这里出去吧，在办公室里吐出来要扣钱的。
t_en: Let's just get out of here. We'll lose money if we end up puking in the office.

t_ch: 地形居然还带变的，筑梦师在设计反入侵装置的时候究竟经历了什么？
t_en: Hold on, the terrain even changes? What were the Dreamweavers thinking when they designed this security system?

t_ch: 这么复杂的结构，公司和反物质军团没防到，反倒把自己人给防了。
t_en: So complicated. They thought they could defend against the IPC or Antimatter Legion, but instead, they just ended up trapping their own people inside.

t_ch: 让我看看又是什么好东西…《匹诺康尼异常记录名册》，听起来不便宜。
t_en: Let's see what we've got this time... List of Anomalies in Penacony. Doesn't sound cheap.

t_ch: 靠！居然是本小说，欺骗我纯真的感情。
t_en: Heck! Can't believe it's just a novel! What a letdown!

t_ch: 呼，可真晕死我了，拿最少的钱，干最累的活。
t_en: Phew, this is a bit too much. Getting such paltry pay for the toughest job.

t_ch: 当然，比起我这微不足道的劳累，还是您最辛苦，请英雄先走。
t_en: But hey, you must be way more exhausted, hero. Go ahead.

t_ch: 这活儿对身体负担也太大了，难怪接单的时候都没人跟我抢。
t_en: This job is simply overwhelming for me. No wonder there were no competitors when I took it on.

t_ch: 最可气的是，外包工还不给买保险，万一有个受伤什么的，连赔偿都拿不到。
t_en: You know what's the most annoying part? We outsourced laborers aren't even covered by insurance. And if we get injured, we won't receive any compensation.

t_ch: 终于找到了，为了这剩下的半截报表，差点连这条小命都没保住。
t_en: Finally, I almost got myself killed for the other half of the document.

t_ch: 英雄，咱们出去把两截报表拼起来吧。
t_en: Let's get out of here and put the two parts together, hero.

t_ch: …宾客，玩吗？
t_en: ...Hey, want to play a game?

t_ch: 玩啥？
t_en: Play what?

t_ch: 嘘，猎犬离太近了，别让他们听见。
t_en: Shhh, the Bloodhounds are close by, we don't want them to hear us.

t_ch: 玩！
t_en: Sure!

t_ch: 嘘，小声点，别让猎犬听见了。
t_en: Shhh, keep it down. We don't want the Bloodhounds to hear us.

t_ch: 不玩
t_en: I'm not interested.

t_ch: 呃…你知道我说的是啥吗？不玩绝对会后悔的。
t_en: Um... Do you know what I'm talking about? You'll definitely regret not playing the game.

t_ch: 如果你收到过我们的通知…就快说出「密码」吧。
t_en: If you've received a message from us... say the password.

t_ch: 滴答滴答滴答！
t_en: Tick-tock, tick-tock, tick-tock!

t_ch: 你看过钟表小子，真不错！但不是这个！
t_en: Impressive! You've watched the Clockie series! But that's not the password!

t_ch: 鸳鸯茶，鸳鸯茶！
t_en: Mandarin Tea! Mandarin Tea!

t_ch: 你爱…不是不是，不是这个！
t_en: So, your favorite tea... No, no. That's not the password!

t_ch: 哼，冲冲冲冲冲！
t_en: Hmhm! Charrrrrrge!

t_ch: 对了，这就是「哈努兄弟」和「仓鼠球骑士」的口头禅，知道这个，你就能拍他们的影片了。
t_en: Right! That's the catchphrase from Brother Hanu and Hamster Ball Knight. Since you know the answer, you'll be allowed to shoot their films.

t_ch: 我给你标好了拍摄区域，设备也给你弄好了，准备好就来吧。
t_en: I've marked the filming area and got the equipment ready. Come when you're ready.

t_ch: 呼——到这里就安全了。
t_en: Phew... We're in a safe area now.

t_ch: 我是影视乐园的工作人员，负责在封闭期间领你们这些游客来体验项目。
t_en: I'm a staff member of Clock Studios Theme Park, and I'll be your guide through the program while the park is closed.

t_ch: 唉，家族说什么安全检查…查到最后搞出了一堆惊梦剧团和忆域迷因，我们珍贵的超大银幕还被不知道什么东西砍成了两半，真是气人！
t_en: The Family said they were doing a security check... but ended up creating tons of Dreamjolt Troupe monsters and Memory Zone memes! And something even chopped our precious mega-screen in half. It's outrageous!

t_ch: 哎呀，抱歉，不该跟客人抱怨这么多的……
t_en: Oops, sorry. I shouldn't complain to guests...

t_ch: 现在这里的《仓鼠球骑士：速度与坚果》和另一侧的《哈努兄弟：狼之道》都开放了，欢迎您随时动工拍摄…呸，体验游玩！
t_en: Now Hamster Ball Knight: Fast & Furynuts and Brother Hanu: Way of the Wolf are available. Please feel free to work... I mean, have fun!

t_ch: 我听到了。
t_en: I heard that.

t_ch: 果然是让我打白工…
t_en: As I thought, you're trying to make me work for free...

t_ch: 呃…您先玩玩再说嘛，我们的设计师已经把影片的制作过程改的和游戏没什么两样了，而且您可以从中得到影视乐园准备的小礼品！
t_en: Well... why not give it a try first? Our designers have turned the movie production process into a game, and you'll get a small gift upon completion!

t_ch: 您收获快乐，我们解决动画排期问题，让更多宾客收获快乐，这不是大好事嘛！
t_en: You'll have fun, we'll solve the animation workload, and other guests will have fun too. Isn't this a perfect solution for everyone?

t_ch: 没什么事的话我就先走了，您玩的开心——别忘了游玩条款，拍摄作品版权归克劳克影业所有哦！
t_en: If there's nothing else, I'll take my leave. Have fun! And don't forget the terms! The copyright of the animations belongs to Clock Studios!

t_ch: 你将开拓小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the Trailbirder on the arcade machine

t_ch: 请摆放心仪的小鸟
t_en: Please place your preferred Bird

t_ch: 开拓小鸟
t_en: Trailbirder

t_ch: 银狼小鸟
t_en: Silbird Wolf

t_ch: 三月小鸟
t_en: March Bird

t_ch: 流萤小鸟
t_en: Birefly

t_ch: 知更小鸟
t_en: Robird

t_ch: 翡翠小鸟
t_en: Jadebird

t_ch: 其他小鸟
t_en: Other Birds

t_ch: 退出
t_en: Exit

t_ch: 返回{TEXTJOIN#87}
t_en: Return to {TEXTJOIN#87}

t_ch: 你将银狼小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the Silbird Wolf on the arcade machine

t_ch: 你将三月小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the March Bird on the arcade machine

t_ch: 你将流萤小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the Birefly on the arcade machine

t_ch: 你将知更小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the Robird on the arcade machine

t_ch: 你将翡翠小鸟摆放在街机上
t_en: You place the Jadebird on the arcade machine

t_ch: 眼前的小鸟轻轻松松叼起比你还大的街机，拍拍翅膀飞走了——
t_en: The little birdie in front of you picks up an arcade machine larger than you with ease and flies away...

t_ch: 嗨，您就是{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}吧？久仰久仰！我是鸢尾花家系的缇娜，之前的邀请函就是我发给您的！
t_en: Hi! You must be {F#Ms.}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}. I'm so happy to finally meet you! I'm Tinah from the Iris Family, the one who invited you!

t_ch: 既然您应邀而来了，那么首先请让我为您简单介绍一下本次的测试内容吧：
t_en: Since you've come, let me briefly explain the test to you:

t_ch: 「折纸小鸟对对碰」是款由苜蓿草家系投资、鸢尾花家系研发制作的集换式街机对战游戏。
t_en: Origami Bird Clash is a multiplayer trading arcade machine battle game developed by the Iris Family, with the Alfalfa Family as investors.

t_ch: 游戏方式很简单：我们现已将用来对战的*街机*投放到了匹诺康尼的各个角落，您需要根据我们提供的*地图*找到它们。
t_en: The gameplay is simple: Arcade machines used for battles have been placed all over Penacony, and you'll have to find them with the help of the map we provide.

t_ch: 被找回的街机将被集中放置在这艘船上，测试者们可在此通过街机进行对战。
t_en: Recovered arcade machines will all be stored on this airship, and testers can use them to play against each other.

t_ch: 好了，更多的内容我就先不赘述了，您还是亲自体验一下吧。
t_en: Alright, I won't bore you any further with more details. After all, it's much more interesting to try it out yourself!

t_ch: 瞧，这是本次活动的初始街机，简称「初号机」。您可以使用本台机器进行身份信息录入，从而获得自己的专属ID卡。
t_en: Now, if you look over here, you'll find the first arcade machine of this event, Unit<unbreak>-01</unbreak>. Register your personal information on it, and you'll receive your own ID card.

t_ch: 没错，就是这样。那么现在，您拥有自己的「专属折纸小鸟」了——如您所见，每位玩家的折纸小鸟，都是根据其形象专门定制生成。
t_en: Exactly like that, yes. You now have your very own Origami Bird. As you can see, the design of every player's Origami Bird is tailored to their likeness.

t_ch: 那么现在，事不宜迟，我们先来进行基础的新手教学吧？
t_en: Shall we get right to the tutorial for newbies now?

t_ch: 我们先去这次的活动场地看看，请随我来！
t_en: Let's take a look at the event venue, come with me!

t_ch: 很好，您已经基本掌握玩法了。那么接下来请根据初号机的内置地图，出发寻找更多的街机，并解锁更多的玩法与功能吧！
t_en: Great! You've mastered the basics. You can now use Unit<unbreak>-01</unbreak>'s built-in map to find more arcade machines and unlock more gameplay and features!

t_ch: 游戏期间如有任何问题或意见，欢迎随时向我反馈。您的支持将会是我们前进的动力。
t_en: If you have any doubts or feedback while playing, please approach me anytime. Your support is what motivates us to do better.

t_ch: 那么接下来，愿您度过一段愉快的时光。
t_en: With that, I wish you an enjoyable gaming session!

t_ch: 呼，总算……
t_en: Phew, finally...

t_ch: 总算走了，这新手教程可真不怎么样……
t_en: She's finally gone. This tutorial kind of sucks...

t_ch: …银狼？
t_en: ...Silver Wolf?

t_ch: 嗨，好久不见，想我了吗？
t_en: Yo! I haven't seen you in a while. Missed me?

t_ch: 你怎么在这里？！
t_en: What are you doing here?

t_ch: 你猜？猜对了就告诉你。
t_en: Take a guess? I'll tell you if you get it right.

t_ch: 不错嘛，很高兴见到你还保持着最基本的警觉…放心吧，我的投影经过了特殊的加密处理，目前只有你能看到。
t_en: Not bad! Glad to see you've still got your wits about you. Don't worry, my hologram is encrypted. You're the only one who can see it right now.

t_ch: 怎么样，看到我这么「咻——」地一下出现在面前，你就没什么想说的吗？
t_en: Well? Don't you have anything to say after seeing me pop up in front of you so suddenly?

t_ch: 你什么时候黑进来的？
t_en: When did you hack your way in?

t_ch: 你猜，猜对了就告诉你。
t_en: Take a guess. I'll tell you if you get it right.

t_ch: 你猜我猜不猜？
t_en: Take a guess if I'm gonna take a guess?

t_ch: 算了，不逗你了——还记得之前给你们打包的隐藏地图作弊代码吗？为了保证星穹列车的诸位不会卡关，我特意在里面加了个小彩蛋。
t_en: Meh, I'll stop teasing. Remember the cheat code for the hidden map I gave you some time back? To ensure that everyone from the Astral Express can clear the stages, I've specially added a small Easter egg inside.

t_ch: 你一直在监视我？
t_en: You've been spying on me?

t_ch: 哈？我是那么无聊的人吗！有这个时间我还不如去养只电子宠物玩玩。
t_en: Huh? Do you think I've got nothing better to do? If I had the time for that, I'd spend it playing with a virtual pet.

t_ch: 还记得之前给你们打包的隐藏地图作弊代码吗？为了保证星穹列车的诸位不会卡关，我特意在里面加了个小彩蛋。
t_en: Remember the cheat code for the hidden map I gave you some time back? To ensure that everyone from the Astral Express can clear the stages, I've specially added a small Easter egg within.

t_ch: 你就这么对我念念不忘？
t_en: You must think of me all the time.

t_ch: …你脑子中病毒了吗？还念念不忘，你又不是我的星际网络账号……
t_en: ...Has your brain been infected with a virus? Me, think of you all the time? Don't kid yourself. It's not like you're my Interastral Network account.

t_ch: 跟你直说吧，还记得之前给你们打包的隐藏地图作弊代码吗？为了保证星穹列车的诸位不会卡关，我特意在里面加了个小彩蛋。
t_en: I'll just get to the point. Remember the cheat code for the hidden map I gave you some time back? To ensure that everyone from the Astral Express can clear the stages, I've specially added a small Easter egg within.

t_ch: 怎么样——惊不惊喜、意不意外？
t_en: Surprise! Didn't see that coming, did you?

t_ch: 你做了什么……
t_en: What did you do?

t_ch: 没必要这么戒备，我要是真想做什么手脚的话早就下手了，也没必要现在出现在你面前。
t_en: Hey now, don't get your hackles up. If I were up to no good, I wouldn't have waited until now to do it, nor would I be appearing before you now.

t_ch: 放心吧，那玩意只能在梦里起作用，而且保质期也差不多就要过了。
t_en: Relax, that thing only works in dreams. Besides, its shelf life will be over soon.

t_ch: 那么现在，我们可以进入正题了吧？
t_en: Can we now move on to more important matters?

t_ch: 我这次来呢，目的很简单，就是为了你在测试的这款「折纸小鸟对对碰」。
t_en: I'm here for a very simple reason: The Origami Bird Clash game you're testing.

t_ch: 这游戏虽然本身玩法无聊的很，还毫无挑战性，来头却并不那么简单——
t_en: Although the game is terribly boring and kind of mindless, the people behind it aren't simple.

t_ch: 公司那帮家伙趁着谐乐大典和家族搭上了线，这事你肯定知道。但我猜你不知道的是，这游戏就是家族为了示好，投给公司的「问路石」。
t_en: I'm sure you're aware that those guys in the IPC made use of the Charmony Festival to hook up with The Family. But I bet you didn't know that this game is The Family's way of testing the waters and a show of goodwill to the IPC.

t_ch: 苜蓿草家系投资、鸢尾花家系研发制作，而公司负责运营——这么有趣的一组COMBO，我会好奇不也合情合理吗？
t_en: The Alfalfa Family invests in the game, the Iris Family develops it, and the IPC manages it. An intriguing combo, don't you think? It's only natural for me to be curious, no?

t_ch: …非常合情合理。
t_en: ...Certainly makes sense.

t_ch: 这么一说我也有点好奇。
t_en: Now that you mention it, I'm starting to get a little curious too.

t_ch: 那既然你同意了，ID卡借我用用，放那就行——
t_en: Well, since you've agreed to it, let me use your ID for a while. Just place it over there and—

t_ch: …我同意什么了？
t_en: ...What did I agree to?

t_ch: 你要我ID卡做什么？
t_en: What do you need my ID for?

t_ch: 当然是和我一起玩游戏啊。我先用你的账号拷贝一份数据……
t_en: To play the game with me, of course. I'll copy the data from your account first...

t_ch: 对，就放那儿…别乱动，马上就好。
t_en: Yes, just place it there... Don't move, it'll take just a minute.

t_ch: 搞定——让我看看，嗯…「根据街机附带的地图寻找线索」…{NICKNAME}你还等什么呢？快点启动「初号机」啊！
t_en: Got it — Let's see, hmm... "Find clues according to the map in the arcade machine"... {NICKNAME}, what are you waiting for? Hurry up and launch Unit<unbreak>-01</unbreak>!

t_ch: 竟然还有线下互动流程，是因为知道原本的玩法过于简单，所以想增加一些跑腿的流程来找补吗…哼，设计思路还真好猜。
t_en: Whoa, there are actually offline interactive processes. Are they making people do in-person fetch quests because they know the game is too simple and want to make up for it? Hmph, it's so easy to tell what they're thinking.

t_ch: 看那边的NPC——就是那些聚在一起的广告牌，那边肯定有东西。
t_en: Look at the NPC over there... Yeah, those billboards gathered together. Something's definitely up with them.

t_ch: 这是什么、是什么！
t_en: Wh—What's this?

t_ch: 好奇怪、奇怪、怪！
t_en: It's s—s—so strange!

t_ch: 快乐美梦速速达——
t_en: Make Your Dreams a Blast...

t_ch: 这么简陋的Demo也好意思拿出来测试，我果真不该抱有任何期待……
t_en: I can't believe they have the nerve to release such a crude demo for testing. Shouldn't have expected better...

t_ch: 准备好了吗？好了就赶紧开始，我倒要看看对战的部分能做成什么样……
t_en: Ready? Then let's get started. I want to see how they designed the combat system...

t_ch: 吵死了啾！好想狠狠揍它们一顿啾！
t_en: Pipe down, tweet! I really want to teach them a lesson, tweet!

t_ch: 呼…终于清净了，啾~
t_en: Phew, finally, some peace and quiet, tweet~

t_ch: 啾——被你发现啦，啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet! You found me! Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 虽说按流程，我现在该给你出「谜题」了啾，不过嘛……
t_en: According to the procedures, I should be giving you a puzzle to solve, but...

t_ch: 谢谢你把我从要了鸟命的噪音里解救出来，就算你合格了啾！
t_en: I'm going to mark you as having cleared it since you saved me from that horrendous noise that could drive even a bird crazy, tweet!

t_ch: 那我先回去啦，啾啾——
t_en: I'll be off then, tweet tweet!

t_ch: …就这？就算是作为Demo，这流程也太简单了吧？
t_en: ...That's it? Isn't this a bit too simple even for a demo?

t_ch: 根本就是个白盒好吗，里里外外都透着敷衍…算了，总之先回去看看街机的内置游戏做得怎么样吧。
t_en: It's pretty much just white-box testing at this rate. Everything is so perfunctory... Forget it, let's just go back and see how the arcade's built-in games are doing for now.

t_ch: 唔…倒是比我想象中要好些，权当消遣放松一下也不错…嗯，你怎么了？
t_en: Uh, it's surprisingly better than I expected it to be. Maybe I can take this as an opportunity to relax and have a break... Hm? What's up with you?

t_ch: …我竟然赢了？
t_en: ...I actually won?

t_ch: 俗话说得好，胜败乃PVP常事，偶尔赢我一局，你也不至于激动得都快掉帧了吧？
t_en: You know what they say about PVP games: You win a few, and you lose a few. No need to get all worked up just because you beat me in an occasional round, is there? You're about to start dropping frames.

t_ch: 不过你这家伙操作意识倒还不错，下次要不要和我一起来打……
t_en: You seem pretty good at handling yourself in games, though. Next time, why don't we play...

t_ch: 锵锵——恭喜您解锁了第一台街机！那么接下来，请让我为您介绍折纸小鸟街机自带的DIY功能！
t_en: Ta-da! Congratulations! You've unlocked the first arcade machine! Now, allow me to show you the Origami Bird Arcade Machine's built-in DIY feature.

t_ch: 在通过街机进行对战并获得胜利后，您的ID卡自动将录入对手的信息，获得对方的折纸小鸟皮肤，甚至*技能*！
t_en: After winning an arcade machine battle, your ID card will automatically save data on your opponent, granting you their Origami Bird skin and abilities!

t_en: In other words, you'll be able to use another player's bird in combat. Doesn't that sound wonderfully exciting?

t_ch: 而除此之外，卡片中存储的小鸟形象还可以通过特殊方式打印出来，请让我为您演示操作方法！
t_en: What's more, by employing a special method, you can even print images of birds saved to your card. Here, allow me to demonstrate!

t_ch: …啧，这破玩意怎么这么高，设计的时候就不能考虑一下皮皮西玩家吗……
t_en: ...Tch, why is this piece of junk so tall? Couldn't they have spared a thought for Pepeshi players when they were designing it?

t_ch: 您看，就是这样——那么您快来试试将您自己的小鸟也摆上去吧！
t_en: See? That's how you do it. Now, try placing your own bird up there!

t_ch: 您做得非常好。事实上，对于DIY模块，我们还设计开发了其它玩法，只是本次测试中尚未实装。
t_en: Nicely done! We actually designed more game modes for the DIY module, but they haven't been implemented. Not for this test, anyway.

t_ch: 为表歉意，刚刚的基础款黄金小鸟就送给您了。
t_en: As amends, you can have this basic Golden Bird you saw earlier.

t_ch: 哈，形象还能自定义？玩法本身虽然还有提升空间，周边系统倒是弄得像模像样的嘛。
t_en: Oh, you can customize the design? The game mode itself could use some work, but I guess the peripheral gameplay systems aren't too shabby.

t_ch: 嗯…这样顺眼多了。
t_en: Yep, that looks so much better.

t_ch: 你那是什么表情…放心，这个投影我也做了信息加密，不会被发现的。
t_en: Why are you looking at me like that? Don't worry, I encrypted the hologram. No one's gonna notice.

t_ch: 凭什么你是初号机？
t_en: Who died and made you Unit<unbreak>-01</unbreak>?

t_ch: …不服？不服也得憋着。
t_en: Don't like it? Well, too bad.

t_ch: 那么我就先不打扰啦，祝您接下来玩得开心~
t_en: Alright, I'll leave you to it now. I hope you enjoy the game!

t_ch: 这个NPC可真生动。要是这次的测试流程能有她一半活泼就好了……
t_en: This NPC is so cheerful. If only the test has some of that vitality too...

t_ch: 街机启动，啾！
t_en: Arcade Machine, it's tweetin' time!

t_ch: 稍等，我还有事。
t_en: Just a moment. I have something else to do.

t_ch: 啾——你好聪明！人家好喜欢你！
t_en: Tweet! You're so clever! I like you!

t_ch: 哇！这就是折纸小鸟吗？好可爱啊——
t_en: Wow! Is this an Origami Bird? It's so cute!

t_ch: 你怎么也在？
t_en: Oh, you're here too?

t_ch: 喂！听听你说的是人话吗——好歹都是一辆车上下来的，我怎么就不能收到邀请了！
t_en: Hey! Rude. We both disembarked from the same train. Of course I got an invite too!

t_ch: 再说了，先前折腾了那么久，我也得歇口气放松一下心情嘛。
t_en: Besides, after everything we went through, I deserve a break to unwind too.

t_en: *sigh* Talking about it is making me all depressed. Why are our vacations all ending in trials and tribulations lately? It's like we're in one of those Xianzhou stories!

t_ch: 还记得我入梦前怎么说的吗？「说不定还会有亲人反目、家破人亡、阴阳两隔、血流成河之类的经典桥段」，没想到就真这么刺激。
t_en: Do you remember what I said before we entered the dream? "Maybe we'll even witness classic scenarios like family feuds, tragic losses, worlds torn apart, espionage situations, rivers running red with blood, stuff like that"? Honestly, who'd have thought that things would really turn out that exciting?

t_en: Everything is the Trailblaze's choice.

t_en: So please, stop jinxing it.

t_en: But since you've brought it up, maybe I should take a lesson or two from Miss Black Swan. Think about it: If I master the art, I can divine our future before we do a warp jump. It'll guarantee a smooth trailblazing expedition for sure!

t_en: ...Black Swan knows the art of divination? I thought that was a Xianzhou thing.

t_en: And the next time we disembark, I'll just set up a roadside stall doing this, and Pom-Pom will never have to worry about our expenses again!

t_ch: 我觉得比起黑天鹅小姐…
t_en: Instead of Miss Black Swan, Back'n & Forth...

t_en: Back'n & Forth might make better mentors for you.

t_en: Tweet! Bad humans, bad! Making a birdie wait! I'm leaving, tweet!

t_en: Sorry, sorry. Don't get your feathers ruffled...

t_ch: 怎么搞得像我欺负鸟似的…算了，要不我们边回船上边说？
t_en: Oh, now I sound like I'm bullying a bird... Forget it. Why don't we talk on the way back to the ship?

t_en: {NICKNAME}! Over here!

t_en: You sure recovered fast. That's some spirit you have.

t_en: That's the Path of Trailblaze for you, I suppose...

t_en: Darn it! No, a pretty girl never gives up! Again! One more round!

t_ch: 还得继续跑流程…
t_en: I have to continue the test...

t_ch: 对了，旁边那两台街机上的折纸小鸟是怎么回事？其中有一只是你的小鸟吧，我的也可以摆上去吗？

t_ch: 这能力简直就像开了挂一样，有机会可以让我研究一下吗？
t_en: That's totally a cheat code if you ask me. Mind letting me look into those abilities of yours sometime?

t_ch: 绕来绕去的我要晕3D了，就不能给个自动寻路吗——
t_en: We've been going around in circles for so long. The 3D graphics are gonna make me sick if this goes on! Why isn't there an auto-navigation feature...

t_ch: 啾——被你发现啦，啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet! You found me! Tweet tweet!

t_ch: 我和拼图先生已经在这里等很久啦！
t_en: Mr. Jigsaw and I have been waiting here forever!

t_ch: 快点快点，我早就准备好了！
t_en: Hurry up! I'm ready to go!

t_en: I was here first, woof!

t_ch: 个鬼啊！知不知道什么叫先来后到！
t_en: Yeah, right! Ever heard of "get in line"!?

t_en: And I was here first, woof!

t_en: You and Silver Wolf are so immersed in watching the drama that neither of you notices the rising pressure in the SoulGlad bottle...
*POP!*

t_ch: 好险…你没事吧，{NICKNAME}？
t_en: That was close... {NICKNAME}, are you okay?

t_en: That was close... Thank you!

t_ch: 不客气，你没事就好。
t_en: Don't mention it. I'm just glad you're okay.

t_en: Mm-hmm, I was actually planning to go to the Moment of Oasis after wandering a little longer. I wasn't expecting to bump into the two of you — and both at the same time, too.

t_ch: 这叫有缘千里来相会！
t_en: Fate brought us together!

t_ch: 嗯，我也觉得和{NICKNAME}非常投缘。
t_en: Yes, I feel like we get along wonderfully too, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 因为银狼对我念念不忘。
t_en: Silver Wolf said she was thinking of me all the time.

t_ch: 没完了是吧！再这样小心你的账号……
t_en: Are we still talking about that!? Don't forget, I can access your account...

t_ch: 不过这里吵成这样，也真亏你待得下去。

t_ch: 人多热闹嘛，看着大家这样有活力，我感觉自己的情绪都稍微高涨起来了。

t_ch: 对了，既然正巧遇到，{NICKNAME}要再来一起拍张合照吗？
t_en: Oh, since we've run into each other... {NICKNAME}, would you like to take another photo together?

t_en: This place makes for a memory worth remembering. Besides, it'll be hard for us to hang out together like this again once we leave the Dreamscape...

t_en: Chill out, you've still got plenty of time before your holiday's over. No need to take that photo right away. That said, since we've run into each other, why don't you join us for some games?

t_en: Games?

t_en: Remember those arcade machines you saw on {TEXTJOIN#87}? The Family plans to develop a new game based on them.

t_en: I'll do my best to protect a pretty young lady!

t_en: Haha, thanks. I appreciate the gesture. Still, is it me, or have I heard that line somewhere else before...?

t_en: Oh, please. Controlling SAM's way harder! You have nothing to worry about. Come on, {NICKNAME}, let's go get that arcade machine. We'll create an account for her after that!

t_ch: 先来后到懂吗！
t_en: Get in line, will you!?

t_en: Um... {NICKNAME}, if it's alright with you, could you play the game with me just a bit?

t_en: You look kind of bored...

t_en: Yeah. I know it's just a demo, but the direction they took for the game does nothing to motivate their players to continue the grind.

t_en: Games like this are only good for casuals... Like that girl who came here on the same train as you.

t_en: Um... {NICKNAME}, if it's alright with you, could you play the game with me just a bit?

t_en: Oh? This NPC sure has a mouth... Okay, so... *disrupt plugin* and done. Let's go back.

t_ch: 都给我滚开啊啾！
t_en: Get out of my way, tweet!

t_en: You're free now, aren't you?

t_en: Forget it. Since you found me, I should stick to the rules, tweet!

t_ch: 这就是你的专属小鸟，根据你的形象生成的那种。你看，那边还有一只我的呢。
t_en: Mm-hmm, this is your exclusive little bird. It was made in your likeness. Look, mine's over there.

t_ch: 好可爱啊…{NICKNAME}、银狼，谢谢你们！我非常喜欢。
t_en: It's so cute. Thank you, {NICKNAME}, Silver Wolf! I love it.

t_ch: 啧啧，你这家伙…倒是很会嘛，难不成还是个恋爱养成游戏老手？
t_en: Tsk tsk, not bad... Could you have been a pro in romance games?

t_ch: 莫要凭空污人清白。
t_en: Nothing of the sort!

t_ch: 真的假的…那要不我给你推荐几款，方便入坑？我只玩精品，绝对保质保量。
t_en: Are you sure...? In that case, shall I recommend a few to start you off? I'm a game connoisseur, so you can rest assured they're all quality recs with plenty of content.

t_ch: 论攻略，我是专业的。
t_en: What can I say? I'm a pro at winning people over.

t_ch: 很自信嘛。那回头我们找款新游戏比一比，看谁先打出全结局呗？
t_en: Feeling confident, aren't we? Then let's pick a new game later and see who clears all the endings first?

t_ch: 恋爱养成游戏…是什么类型的游戏？要不也带我一起玩吧？
t_en: Romance games... what kind of games are those? Can you show me how to play them?

t_ch: 咳…不说闲话了。{NICKNAME}，你该干什么就干什么去吧，我还得带流萤继续特训呢。
t_en: Ahem... Let's get back to business. Feel free to go on ahead without us, {NICKNAME}. I'm staying with Firefly to continue her special training.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}？没想到能在这里遇见。你也是来参加那个折纸小鸟游戏测试的吗？
t_en: {NICKNAME}? I didn't think I would be able to meet you here. Are you here to test the game about Origami Birds too?

t_ch: 他们连你都请来了？
t_en: They even invited you?

t_ch: 我最近刚好想要稍微散散心，就过来看看。
t_en: I felt like I needed a change of pace lately, so I decided to pop by.

t_ch: 你也喜欢玩游戏？
t_en: You like playing video games too?

t_ch: 不…其实我并不擅长玩游戏，只是最近想要稍微散散心，就过来看看。
t_en: Not really... Games aren't my strong suit, to be honest. But I felt like I needed a change of pace lately, so I decided to pop by.

t_ch: 我记得{NICKNAME}你应该很擅长游戏吧？毕竟每次看到你时都是手机不离手…那接下来，我可以和你一起吗？
t_en: If I recall correctly, {NICKNAME}, you're quite the expert at games, right? After all, I've never seen you without your phone in your hands. Do you mind if I join you?

t_ch: 那句话是怎么说的来着…「大佬，请带带我」——是这样的没错吧？
t_en: How do they say it again? ...Oh, I remember now. "Carry me, please!" Did I get it right?

t_ch: 唔…真的好像啊……
t_en: Ah... So similar...

t_ch: 像什么？
t_en: What is?

t_ch: 在银河间巡演的时候，曾有位朋友送了我一本童谣。故事的主角是位没有名字的勇者。
t_en: Back when I was on a cosmic tour, a friend once gave me a children's book. The protagonist of the story was a hero without a name.

t_ch: 那位勇者肩负着打败魔王、拯救公主的使命，四处冒险、走遍了大陆的每一个角落。
t_en: Tasked with the duty of vanquishing the demon lord and rescuing the princess, the hero walked every corner of the land, going on adventure after adventure.

t_ch: 旅途中他不时会遇到一种调皮的小精灵。它们喜欢捉迷藏，被找到时还会发出某种奇怪的笑声，就像刚刚的小鸟一样……
t_en: During the journey, he occasionally encounters mischievous little sprites. They enjoy playing hide and seek, and when found, they emit a strange laughter, just like the little bird from earlier...

t_ch: 虽是给小孩子看的童谣，但那本书的情节非常精彩动人。一路上，勇者遇到了许多的神奇生物，也见到了各异的美丽风景。
t_en: Although it's a tale for children, the story is incredibly exciting and engaging. During his journey, the hero encountered so many fantastic creatures and witnessed so many unique and breathtaking places.

t_ch: 他在草原上驰骋、攀登雪峰、穿越沙漠、甚至曾抵达过炙热的地心……
t_en: He rode across fields, scaled snowy peaks, and crossed deserts, even reaching as far as the fiery core of the planet...

t_ch: 那勇者最后打败魔王了吗？
t_en: Did he vanquish the demon lord in the end?

t_ch: 那勇者最后救出公主了吗？
t_en: Did he rescue the princess in the end?

t_ch: 唔…大概吧。奇怪，那本书我曾反复读过好几遍，如今却记不清故事的结局了。
t_en: Hm... I think so? Strange, I've read that book so many times, but I can't seem to recall how it ends.

t_ch: 但和勇者一同冒险的那种身临其境的感受，直到今天仍然记忆犹新。
t_en: At any rate, that feeling of immersion like I'm still adventuring with the hero is still fresh in my mind even now.

t_ch: 就好像一首经典的歌曲一样，即便已然忘却准确的歌词曲调，可曲中传达的某种心绪、情感、企望，已然深植于人们的心间……
t_en: It's like listening to a classic song. Even if the lyrics and melody have become fuzzy, it doesn't change how the emotions, nuances, and feelings of longing expressed in the song have engraved themselves on the listener's heart...

t_ch: 啊，抱歉，耽误得似乎有些久了…我们现在是不是该回去了？
t_en: Ah, I'm sorry! I held you up for a while, didn't I? Is it time we head back?

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，你终于回来了！快快快，和我来一局，这次我定要一雪前耻！
t_en: You're finally back, {NICKNAME}! Hurry up and play a match with me. This time around, I will avenge my previous loss!

t_ch: 咦…原来还有其他人在，这位小姐是？
t_en: Huh... There are other people here as well. Who are you, miss?

t_ch: 是我，三月小姐。好久不见，很高兴见到你。
t_en: It's me, Miss March. It's good to see you again.

t_ch: 这个声音…难道是知——咳、咳咳咳！
t_en: This voice... sounds like Ro— *cough, cough cough*!

t_ch: 梦里就是这点好，无论外貌还是嗓音，乔装起来都非常方便。
t_en: This is why dreams are such wonderful things — it's so convenient to disguise myself, from my appearance to my voice.

t_ch: 确实方便，我刚刚根本没认出来，哈哈…所以知更鸟小姐也是来这里测试游戏的吗！我觉得这个游戏超好玩诶……
t_en: So convenient that I couldn't even recognize you at all, haha... So, are you also here to test out the game, Miss Robin? I think it's a really fun one...

t_ch: 是啊，我刚刚和{NICKNAME}玩了一局，感觉还挺有趣的。
t_en: Yes, I just played a match with {NICKNAME} and it was indeed quite interesting.

t_ch: 哇，有这种好事{NICKNAME}你竟然不叫我！我也想和知更鸟小姐一起玩…所以你们谁赢了？
t_en: Wha... I can't believe you didn't tell me about this, {NICKNAME}! I want to play the game with Miss Robin too... So, who won in the end?

t_ch: 当然是我！
t_en: I did, of course!

t_ch: 呵，少嘚瑟，待会先来与我大战三百回合！
t_en: Hah, don't be so cocky. Later, let's have a one-on-one battle!

t_ch: 运气好罢了。
t_en: I was just lucky.

t_ch: 对我怎么不见你这么谦虚，待会先来与我大战三百回合！
t_en: You're just pretending to be humble. Later, let's have a one-on-one battle!

t_ch: 对了，{NICKNAME}，你先把知更鸟小姐的折纸小鸟摆上，给我看……
t_en: Oh right, {NICKNAME}, you should place Miss Robin's Origami Bird first, so that I can take a look at it...

t_ch: 嗨——嗨！各位，我又来了——请大家一同恭喜{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}！
t_en: Hello! Ladies and gentlemen, it's me again... Please join me in congratulating {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 既然您已率先集齐了四台街机，那么请让我为您送上本次测试的纪念奖励——「折纸小鸟街机·限定豪华款」！
t_en: Since you managed to collect four arcade machines first, allow me to give you the souvenir for this test: A limited-edition deluxe version of the Origami Bird Arcade Machine!

t_ch: 那么，在本次测试流程的最后，请允许我向诸位介绍一位贵客——
t_en: Well then, as we come to the end of the test, allow me to introduce a guest to all of you...

t_ch: 今天的熟面孔可真不少…没想到这么快就又见面了，诸位。
t_en: There are quite a number of familiar faces here today... I didn't expect to meet all of you again so soon.

t_ch: 呃，你好，翡翠女士，好巧啊……
t_en: Ah, hello, Madam Jade. What a coincidence...

t_ch: 你好，翡翠女士。
t_en: Hello, Madam Jade.

t_ch: 我原本只是顺路替托帕跑一趟，验收下项目进度，倒是未曾想还有这惊喜……
t_en: I was only intending to do Topaz a favor and check on the progress of this project. I certainly didn't expect to be surprised as well...
Umm...

t_ch: 难得这么热闹，我不妨也来添个彩头。
t_en: Since there's such a lively crowd, let's give them something to cheer about.

t_ch: 这位小姐，可以请你替我陪他们玩一局吗？
t_en: Miss, would you play a match on my behalf?

t_ch: 咦？当、当然没问题，翡翠女士！
t_en: Eh? O—Of course, Madam Jade!

t_ch: 厉害厉害，{NICKNAME}{F#小姐}{M#先生}，难怪您能在测试中拔得头筹！
t_en: How impressive! No wonder you were able to come in first during the test, {F#Miss}{M#Mr.} {NICKNAME}!

t_ch: 那么恭喜，现在这只美丽优雅、娴静端庄的小鸟属于您了！不赶紧摆出来看看吗？
t_en: In that case, my heartiest congratulations, for this beautifully elegant and quietly dignified bird now belongs to you! Are you not going to place it right now?

t_ch: 在缇娜闪闪发光的企盼眼神中，你将翡翠小鸟摆上了最后的街机。
t_en: As Tinah looks on, her eyes sparkling with anticipation, you place the Jadebird on the final arcade machine.

t_ch: 完美！非常完美——好啦，那么眼看着本次测试就要接近尾声了，诸位要不要一起拍照留个纪念？
t_en: Perfect! Utterly perfect... Alright, seeing as the test is coming to an end, shall we take a group photo to commemorate this moment?

t_ch: 拍照吗？好呀好呀，带我一个！知…你要来吗？
t_en: A photo? Sure thing, count me in! Ro... *ahem*, are you joining in?

t_ch: 好啊，我也没问题的。
t_en: Fine by me.

t_ch: （向流萤招手）
t_en: (Wave at Firefly)

t_ch: 诶…是让我也一起吗？
t_en: Huh... I can be in the photo, too?

t_ch: 去呗，之前你不就想和{NICKNAME}合照吗。
t_en: Go on, didn't you want to take a photo with {NICKNAME} earlier?

t_ch: 那…好吧。
t_en: Okay then...

t_ch: 嗯，那…翡翠女士，您呢？
t_en: Um... how about you, Madam Jade?

t_ch: 我就不必了吧，毕竟…这地方的人际关系有些复杂呢。万一照片被其他人看到的话，那影响可不太好。
t_en: That won't be necessary. After all... the social dynamics of this place are quite complicated. It would be rather unfortunate if the photo is seen by others.

t_ch: 噢？噢…理解，理解。那么，除了翡翠女士以外的各位，请移步这边……
t_en: Oh? Oh... I see. In that case, apart from Madam Jade, would everybody come over to this side...

t_ch: 很好，那么我宣布——本次「折纸小鸟对对碰」白盒测试正式告一段落！
t_en: Perfect! I hereby announce that the Origami Bird Clash white-box testing has officially come to an end!

t_ch: …哈，我就说吧，果然就是个白盒……
t_en: Ha, told you! It was totally a white-box test...

t_ch: 非常感谢各位的参与，后续我们将根据测试数据及获得的意见反馈，对游戏进行更深度的打磨，敬请期待！
t_en: Everybody, thank you very much for participating. We'll use the data gathered from the test and the feedback provided by you to improve the game even further. Please look forward to the final release!

t_ch: 此外，已投放的样机将作为纪念品留在大厅里，本次测试结束后依旧可以使用哦。
t_en: In addition, the used prototypes will be kept in the hall as souvenirs, so you'll still be able to access them even after the end of this test.

t_ch: 什么，还可以继续玩吗？好耶——
t_en: What? We can continue to play the game? Yay!

t_ch: 没错！并且这些街机中已录入本次测试玩家的对战数据，能够生成更高强度的关卡供大家挑战……
t_en: Indeed! Not only that, the battle data of all players who participated in this test have been recorded in these arcade machines, allowing them to generate even more difficult stages for all of you to challenge...

t_ch: 呦呵，看来那边还有DLC啊？
t_en: Yo, looks like there's even DLC over there?

t_ch: 翡翠女士，她是不是……
t_en: Madam Jade, did she...

t_ch: 嗯，她估计早就发现了。 不过问题不大，我们这就要溜了——对了，你不是说想去「绿洲的时刻」吗？反正我现在也无聊得很，干脆和你一起好了。
t_en: Yes, she probably found out about us long ago. However, it's no big deal, for it's time for us to slip away... Right, didn't you mention that you wanted to visit the Moment of Oasis? Since I have nothing else to do right now, I'll go with you.

t_ch: 既然这样…机会难得，要不要去日光公园逛逛？我还从来没和人一起「野餐」过…嗯，出任务期间那种不算。而且梦里的公园应该没有昆虫吧？
t_en: In that case... since this is a rare opportunity, shall we visit Sunshine Park? I've never had a picnic with another person before... Well, if you don't count missions. Also, the parks in dreams shouldn't have any insects, right?

t_ch: 行呗，那我们就走了，{NICKNAME}。晚点找你联机，记得把时间给我空出来。
t_en: Alright. In that case, we're out of here, {NICKNAME}. I'll contact you again later, so remember to leave some time for me.

t_ch: 再见，{NICKNAME}！谢谢你的邀请，我今天很开心！
t_en: Goodbye, {NICKNAME}! Thank you for inviting me, I had a lot of fun today!

t_ch: 既然如此，我也先告辞啦，祝各位度过愉快的一天！
t_en: Well, it's also time for me to bid farewell. Have a pleasant day, everybody!

t_ch: 唉，这么快人就都走光了，果然快乐的时光总是短暂的……
t_en: Ugh, all of them left so quickly. Time flies when you're having fun, indeed...

t_ch: 不过你可不许走，{NICKNAME}。刚刚缇娜小姐跟我说了街机的新玩法，你快来陪我试一试！
t_en: Anyway, you're not allowed to go yet, {NICKNAME}. Miss Tinah just told me about a new game mode for the arcade machines, so you've got to try it out with me!

t_ch: …这非常有趣，知更鸟小姐。那么如果你需要，我这次依旧可以提供一些小小的帮助。
t_en: How interesting, Miss Robin. I can still aid you, if you require any help on my end.

t_ch: …我会考虑的，翡翠女士。但无论如何，非常感谢你的善意。
t_en: I will think about it, Madam Jade. Regardless, I appreciate your kindness very much.

t_ch: 无妨。时间还早，我们不如换个地方好好聊聊。难得恰好彼此都有空闲，不是吗。
t_en: It's not a big deal. Since it's still early, why don't we chat somewhere else? After all, you have time to spare, right?

t_ch: 当然，我正巧知道一家不错的咖啡厅，环境优美、而且非常清净，或许你会喜欢……
t_en: Of course. I happen to know a decent café that is spotless and overlooks some beautiful scenery. Perhaps you'll like it...

t_ch: 啾啾——这边啾啾啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet — this way, tweet tweet!

t_ch: 关于「折纸小鸟」，匹诺康尼一直都流传着这样的说法：这些卡通角色其实是真实存在的，但只有被它们认可的人才能看见。
t_en: When it comes to Origami Birds, there has always been an urban legend spreading around Penacony: That these cartoon characters are actually real, but can only be seen by people whom they approve of.

t_ch: 所以当这些小鸟出现在眼前时，我不由产生了一种非常奇妙的感觉……
t_en: Therefore, when these birds appeared before my eyes, an intense feeling of marvel welled up inside me...

t_ch: 对了，{NICKNAME}，待会可以稍微等等我吗？
t_en: Oh, right. {NICKNAME}, can you wait a while for me later?

t_ch: 在回去船上之前，我可能需要做些简单的变装。
t_en: Before I return to the ship, I may need to disguise myself for a bit.

t_ch: 毕竟在梦里，人们本就该能够随心所欲呢…对了，{NICKNAME}，我其实并不擅长这种游戏，你可以教教我吗？
t_en: Of course, in dreams, people should be able to do as they please... Oh, by the way, {NICKNAME}, I'm actually not very good at these games. Can you teach me?

t_ch: 我已经准备好了哦，{NICKNAME}。
t_en: I'm ready, {NICKNAME}.

t_ch: 客人您准备好了的话，随时都可以开始哦。
t_en: You can start anytime you're ready, esteemed guests.

t_ch: 啾啾！来陪我玩捉迷藏啾~
t_en: Tweet tweet! Come play hide and seek with me, tweet!

t_ch: 啾啾啾——被你们发现了啾！
t_en: Tweet tweet! You got me, tweet!

t_ch: 我玩得很开心，谢谢啾~
t_en: This is fun, thank you, tweet!

t_ch: 这…你这变装变得连种族都不一样了啊？
t_en: This... you've changed so much that even your species is different now?

t_ch: 您来了，来来来，我给您介绍下，这位是我们科的新人——
t_en: You're here! Come, come. Let me introduce to you the latest addition to our department —

t_ch: 噢，您就是传闻中的{NICKNAME}啊，久仰大名！我是梅透娜，刚来空间站不久，以后请多指教。
t_en: Oh, you're the {NICKNAME} that everyone's been talking about. It's an honor! I'm Metauna. I only just arrived at the space station. It's nice to meet you.

t_ch: 所以…你们找我有事吗？
t_en: So... you were looking for me?

t_ch: 是这样的，之前世玲姐丢给我的一套战斗模拟工具，名字叫「星芒战幕」……
t_en: It's about a combat simulation tool that Shiling shoved in my hands. It's called the Stellar Flare...

t_ch: 有点耳熟，好像我还测试过。
t_en: Sounds familiar. I think I've tested it before.

t_ch: 对对对，就是我喊您来帮忙的，当时多亏有您！
t_en: That's right. I was the one who called for you to help. Good thing you were around that time!

t_ch: 那是什么？
t_en: What's that?

t_ch: 这是一套作战模拟软件，旨在提高使用者的战斗水平。
t_en: This is a combat simulation software. It's supposed to boost its user's combat proficiency.

t_ch: 早期开发版的测试结果挺好的，在安保科的评估中，他们甚至觉得该程序有交付给「公司」那边商业化的潜力。
t_en: Early prototype test results were encouraging. The Security Department even expected that this program had the potential to be submitted to the IPC for business applications.

t_ch: 最次也能留作空间站内部安防训练使用。
t_en: At worst, it would be kept on the space station for training purposes.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，小娜说的没错，我们科室挺看重它的。
t_en: Yes, yes. Metauna's right on the nose. We feel really good about this project.

t_ch: 所以咱们科就派了我过来给前辈搭把手，希望我俩能优化下这套工具。基于需求，我给这一版的程序取了个新名字叫「星芒烁变」…哈哈，新版本新气象。
t_en: Which is why the department sent me to assist you, in the hopes that we can optimize this tool. As per the requirements, I gave this version of the program a new name — Stellar Shimmer... Haha, new version, new beginnings!

t_ch: 不过说实话，我是没什么信心啦，一上来就接触这么大的项目…这压力我有点吃不消。
t_en: But to be honest, I'm feeling really anxious about this. To be involved in such a huge project so soon after I arrived... I think the stress is a bit too much.

t_ch: 别慌，现在原始代码已经迭代过一版了，我们对齐了安保科的需求，优化了模拟战斗的流程……
t_en: Keep calm. The source code's already been iterated once. We've also cross-checked with the Security Department's requirements and optimized the simulated combat's process...

t_ch: 咱们科室的黑话就打住吧，凯辛姐，还是说正事吧。
t_en: Enough of internal jargon, Grumpy. We should get down to business.

t_ch: 咳咳，不好意思，这是职业病了…总之，程序优化好了，但还差个精通战斗的高手来完成测试。所以我就找了您过来，有些冒昧，但空间站里身手了得的，我第一反应就是您了……
t_en: Ahem, sorry. Work habits... In any case, the program has been optimized, but it still lacks a combat master to complete its testing. That's why we sought you out. It's a bit sudden, but you were the first person I thought of when it comes to battle prowess superiority on this space station...

t_ch: {NICKNAME}，您能否赏脸来帮我们这个忙？
t_en: {NICKNAME}, will you be so kind as to help us out?

t_ch: 既然如此…我也不好拒绝。
t_en: If that's the case... I'd be hard-pressed to refuse.

t_ch: 义不容辞，这种事找我就对了。
t_en: Just part of the job. You've come to the right person.

t_ch: 太好啦，有高手帮忙，肯定能成！
t_en: Fantastic! With an expert helping out, this'll surely be a success!

t_ch: 程序已经装载在旁边的终端上了，测试的事…那就拜托您了！
t_en: The program has been loaded on the terminal by the side. As for the testing... We're counting on you!

t_ch: 启动模拟程序
t_en: Launch simulation program.

t_ch: {NICKNAME}大神来了！
t_en: The awesome {NICKNAME} is here!

t_ch: 怎么样，您已经全部测完了吗？
t_en: How was it? Did you finish all the testing?

t_ch: 全部通关了，小菜一碟。
t_en: I cleared all the levels, and it was a cinch.

t_ch: 我就知道，不愧是您！
t_en: I knew it. There's no one better!

t_ch: 有点棘手，还好我技高一筹。
t_en: It got a little hairy. Good thing I was a little stronger.

t_ch: 您觉得棘手？唔…看来难度设置不太合理，连您都这么说了，其他人肯定吃不消。怪我，我回去重新调试下相应的数值好了。
t_en: It was difficult for you? Oof... I guess the difficulty setting is still too harsh. If you're saying that, others would surely get stomped. My bad, I'll have to go back and tweak the relevant values.

t_ch: 真的很感谢您，这次帮了我们一个大忙！我刚刚看了下后台上传的数据，感觉没什么大问题…等我们再调试下，通过验收不在话下！
t_en: I really must thank you, as you've helped us out greatly this time! I just took a look at the uploaded back-end data, and nothing seemed amiss... Wait till we're done with the adjustments, and it'll definitely clear inspection!

t_ch: 提交回去，其他前辈估计会大吃一惊——然后推给「公司」那边搞商业化，不出五年，不不不，搞不好一经上市就会大受欢迎——再之后我的考评，我的研究经费，嘿嘿……
t_en: When it's submitted, my seniors will probably be flabbergasted. And then they'll recommend it to the IPC for commercial applications. Within five years, no no, it may even be a surefire hit once it gets released on the market! And then my assessment, and my research funding, hee-hee...

t_ch: 咳咳，小娜，现实点。先给我们的大救星道谢吧，托您的福，这项目未来可期——所以我给您准备了一些小礼物，希望您能收下。
t_en: Ahem, Metauna, let's keep the daydreaming for later. I'd thank our savior first — it's all because of you that this project will have a bright future. I've prepared some token gifts for you. Please take them.

t_ch: 嗯嗯，真的很感谢您！
t_en: Yes yes, thank you so much!

t_ch: 测试的事…就拜托您了，如果测试过程中遇到什么问题，请随时知会我。
t_en: We're counting on you for the test. If you run into any problems during its course, please feel free to contact me.

t_ch: 我们一定改！
t_en: We'll definitely change it for the better!

t_ch: 这位叫「铄金」的对手，似乎是个修行仙舟古武术的格斗家。
t_en: Our opponent, Shuojin, appears to be a fighter using Xianzhou ancient martial arts.

t_ch: 我查了查他的对战记录，不算是个特别厉害的选手。你应该可以放心接下挑战。
t_en: His combat history suggests he's not particularly formidable. You should be able to take on this challenge with confidence.

t_ch: 我了解了。不过，不管是什么样的对手，我都不会掉以轻心的。
t_en: Understood. Still, I never underestimate any opponent.

t_ch: 另外…你真的不打算考虑一下和公司的商务合作吗？
t_en: Also... Are you sure you won't reconsider partnering with the IPC for business?

t_ch: 其实也不是不能考虑。
t_en: It's not entirely off the table.

t_ch: 我不知道…我个人不想答应这件事，但我也不知道断然拒绝是不是一件好事……
t_en: I'm not sure... I personally don't want to agree to it, but I don't know if it's wise to refuse it outright.

t_ch: 这真的太危险了。
t_en: This is far too risky.

t_ch: 我也觉得。我毕竟来自小地方，实在搞不懂这些大势力的种种套路……
t_en: I agree. Coming from a small town, I find the intricate games these big players play are just beyond me...

t_ch: 和你合作不就算和公司合作吗？
t_en: Doesn't working with you essentially mean collaborating with the IPC?

t_ch: 这肯定不一样啊！我现在的身份不过是一个体育记者，和卢卡选手之间谈不上有什么商务往来。
t_en: It's completely different! I'm just a sports journalist, and I don't have any business dealings with Contestant Luka.
